Find Sherpas Boutique Search Agency
Search Quality Report

60 Woodie's Searches. 25 Revenue-Risk Moments.

An independent audit of how Woodie's search handles product names, brands, filters, and shopper intent.
Prepared by Michal Pekarcik · Find Sherpas

A sample of the 60 real customer queries we tested

kärcher home summer garden care colurtrend deck staining project wood stain exterior wood stain brown chain saws lawnmower dulux paint how to clean my patio quickly dewalt tools trimmer makita dulux white paint dulx paint diy tools lawnmowers paintoffers colourtrend grass cutters lawnmower under 300 lawn bbq deals 30% off bbq lawn mowers
Report Crossroads

Jump to a section

Search risk at a glance → Tested query patterns → Improvement map → Advanced diagnostics → Prioritized roadmap → Revenue-risk failures → Detailed analysis → Complete test results → Scope and boundaries →
Summary

Search risk at a glance

The short version

We tested 60 realistic customer queries against Woodie's's search, live on site. The audit checks whether customers see a relevant top result, not just whether the search engine returns something.

This report focuses on the 25 queries where a customer likely hits a dead end: an off-topic result, a missing interpretation, a dropped constraint, or a relevant product buried too far down.

The first fix to ship: Implement brand priority boost for brand-specific queries — Fixes Brand & Model Search failures where customers searching "makita" or "flymo lawnmowers" see competitor brands instead of the requested brand.

42%
revenue risk. 25 of 60 tested queries cause a likely customer bounce today. These are queries where a customer sees a dead end: no relevant top result, wrong interpretation, or the right product buried too far down.
25
Revenue risk
Off-topic top result, missing interpretation, or severe ranking failure. The customer leaves.
31
Friction
The query is close, but still creates work: the best match is not surfaced cleanly enough.
4
Working well
Top result is relevant and ranking is near-optimal. No immediate attention needed.
70%
Right products found, ranked too low
#5
Avg. best result position
47%
Best result outside top 3
15%
Irrelevant #1 result
Most severe example A customer searching "hp lawnmower" sees "Pro Lawn Self Propelled Electric Start Hyundai Lawnmower 53cm" at the #1 spot — not what they asked for.
Pattern Calibration Map

Where each query family needs tuning

These are diagnostic probes, not grades. Each row shows the strength of the tuning signal in that sample, separating severe shopper-visible misses from minor ranking polish.

Product Discovery
Direct match
Exact-match polish
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “bosch garden powertools” — The highest relevance result 'Bosch Isio Shape & Edge 3.6V Cordless Grass and Shrub Shear' (0.676 relevance) was buried at position #9, while lower-relevance…
Can customers find an exact product when they already know the name?
Next tuning move: Protect exact product-title matches and verify they are not outranked by broader keyword matches.
Product Discovery
Broad category
Collection routing
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 stable result.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “garden” — The highest relevance result '750Ml Garden And Wood Paint Juniper Burst' (0.590 relevance) was buried at position #15, while lower relevance items like…
Does search understand high-level shopping terms and collection-style queries?
Next tuning move: Map common retail terms to collections, category pages, and merchandising rules.
Typo Tolerance
Typo
Typo recovery
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “colurtrend” — The search engine successfully handled the typo 'colurtrend' and retrieved all relevant Colourtrend products, but ranked them poorly.
Does search recover when brand or product terms are misspelled?
Next tuning move: Add fuzzy matching for high-value brands, product names, and common category terms.
Typo Tolerance
Merged words
Pattern tuning
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “paintoffers” — The highest relevance result 'Ronseal Ultimate Protection Decking Paint Deep Blue 2.5L' (0.488 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower relevance…
This probe checks whether search handles this customer query pattern predictably.
Next tuning move: Review the failed examples and add this pattern to the search relevance regression set.
Typo Tolerance
Split word
Pattern tuning
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 2 polish notes, 1 stable result.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “lawn mowers” — The search engine correctly retrieved relevant lawn mower products but ranked them poorly.
This probe checks whether search handles this customer query pattern predictably.
Next tuning move: Review the failed examples and add this pattern to the search relevance regression set.

Special character
Pattern tuning

Example “black+decker” — The highest relevance result 'Black+Decker 300W Oscillating Multi Tool & Accessories' (0.816 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower relevance…
This probe checks whether search handles this customer query pattern predictably.

Typo Tolerance
Partial query
Autocomplete intent
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 2 friction points, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “lawn” — The two highest relevance products 'Hand Push Lawn Mower, 40cm Cutting Width' (0.652) and 'Hand Push Lawn Mower, 30cm Cutting Width' (0.652) were buried at…
Do incomplete typed-ahead queries surface plausible products before the full term is entered?
Next tuning move: Tune prefix matching and autocomplete-backed retrieval for short or incomplete terms.
Language Understanding
Synonym
Vocabulary mapping
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “wood stain” — The highest relevance product 'Ronseal 2.5 Litre Quick Drying Woodstain Satin Dark Oak' (0.844 relevance) was ranked #15 instead of #1.
Does search connect customer vocabulary to the store's preferred product wording?
Next tuning move: Expand synonym dictionaries and semantic embeddings for the product language customers actually use.
Language Understanding
Natural language
Intent translation
3 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 friction point.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “how to clean my patio quickly” — The query 'how to clean my patio quickly' should return pressure washers or patio cleaning equipment, but instead returned completely unrelated items: #1 'BG…
Can longer, conversational needs be translated into product intent?
Next tuning move: Extract intent phrases from longer queries before ranking products.
Brand & Model Search
Brand search
Brand protection
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “makita” — Customer searched for 'makita' but all 15 results are DeWalt products.
Are brand requests recognized and protected from unrelated results?
Next tuning move: Detect brand entities and boost or filter to products from the requested brand.
Brand & Model Search
Abbreviation
Pattern tuning
4 probes reviewed: 1 friction point, 2 polish notes, 1 stable result.
The pattern works in places, but the experience is not consistently clean.
Example “diy tools” — The search engine retrieved relevant DIY tools but ranked them poorly.
This probe checks whether search handles this customer query pattern predictably.
Next tuning move: Review the failed examples and add this pattern to the search relevance regression set.
Shopping Context
Plural / singular
Term normalization
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 2 friction points, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “accessories” — The highest relevance result 'D-Line Accessory Pack 16X08 - Black' (0.727) was buried at position #10, while much lower relevance results dominated the top…
Does search normalize simple wording variants such as singular and plural terms?
Next tuning move: Normalize singular, plural, and close inflection variants before retrieval.
Shopping Context
Seasonal / occasion
Occasion matching
3 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “spring lawn preparation” — The query 'spring lawn preparation' should return lawn care products, but the most relevant result 'Evergreen Autumn Lawn Care Spreader 80m2' (relevance 0.318)…
Can seasonal or occasion-based needs map to relevant categories and products?
Next tuning move: Add occasion and season tags, then use them as ranking signals for broad needs.
Shopping Context
Use case
Pattern tuning
3 probes reviewed: 1 friction point, 1 polish note, 1 stable result.
The pattern works in places, but the experience is not consistently clean.
Example “small garden maintenance” — The query 'small garden maintenance' describes a use case requiring maintenance tools and supplies, but the engine only matched the literal word 'small' in…
This probe checks whether search handles this customer query pattern predictably.
Next tuning move: Review the failed examples and add this pattern to the search relevance regression set.
Filters & Constraints
Multi-attribute
Constraint stacking
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 2 friction points, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “electric hedge trimmer lightweight” — The highest relevance result 'Einhell GC-CH 18/40 Li Solo Cordless Hedge Trimmer' (0.688 relevance) was buried at position #14, while much lower relevance…
Can search keep multiple requirements active at the same time?
Next tuning move: Score each constraint independently so one matched term does not drown out the rest.
Filters & Constraints
Price anchored
Price parsing
3 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “budget paint brushes” — The query 'budget paint brushes' included a price constraint ('budget') but the search engine completely ignored it.
Can price constraints be recognized and used as a ranking or filtering signal?
Next tuning move: Parse numeric price limits and connect them to product price metadata.

Advanced Diagnostics

What the visible result set reveals

These checks stay inside the same query → results → audit boundary, but look beyond the single best result.

Result Set Purity

This checks how much of the visible result set is actually relevant, not just whether one good result exists somewhere. A noisy top 5 means customers must visually filter around weak results even when search technically found a match.

58%
top 5 relevant

Result

The visible result set is noisy: only 58% of top 5 results were relevant on average, and top 10 purity was 56%.

Evidence

  • "flymo lawnmowers": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "garden": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "tools": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "home": 0/5 relevant in top 5

How to remedy it

Tune ranking to demote low-relevance partial matches once a strong intent match exists. Add a top-N quality guardrail: for each important query class, require a minimum share of relevant results in the top 5, not only a good best-result position.

Top Result Trust

This asks whether the first result is defensible as the best thing to show a customer. A bad #1 is the clearest search failure because it shapes the shopper's first impression.

40%
questionable #1 results

Result

The first result is not reliable enough: 24 of 60 queries (40%) had a weak, partial, or clearly beaten #1 result.

Evidence

  • "how to clean my patio quickly": #1 was "BG Single to Double Socket Convertor" (score 0.22). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "spring lawn preparation": #1 was "Spring Seasonal Bowl 35cm" (score 0.26). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "tools": #1 was "Lay-Z-Spa Miami Airjet Hot Tub" (score 0.27). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "home": #1 was "Aquaplus Xpanding Magic Hose Kit 5M-15M" (score 0.33). The first result is weakly related to the query.

How to remedy it

Add a stricter first-result threshold for high-intent queries. If the top result is weak and a stronger result exists lower, boost the stronger exact/category/brand match or suppress the weak partial match from rank #1.

Attribute Drift

This decomposes multi-attribute queries to see which terms survive into the top results. It distinguishes right-category-but-wrong-attribute failures from total search failures.

6
queries with attribute loss

Result

6 multi-attribute queries showed some attribute loss in the top 5. The audit separates preserved, partially preserved, and dropped terms so the team can see which part of the query is being lost.

Evidence

  • "budget paint brushes": dropped budget, brushe; partially preserved none
  • "bosch cordless garden tools": dropped garden, tool; partially preserved none
  • "exterior wood stain brown": dropped exterior, brown; partially preserved none
  • "lawnmower under 300": dropped 300; partially preserved none

How to remedy it

Extract query attributes before ranking and give required attributes explicit weight. For brand, size, material, activity, and style terms, penalize results that match the product type but omit or contradict the attribute.

Diversity vs Redundancy

This checks whether broad or exploratory queries show useful choice, or whether the top results are flooded by repeated variants. It is most useful for multi-brand or broad-catalog ecommerce, and less useful for true single-brand stores.

6
unique families in worst broad top 10

Result

The most redundant broad result set was "deck staining project": 6 unique product families across 10 top results.

Evidence

  • "deck staining project": 6 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "paint": 7 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "garden": 10 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "tools": 6 unique product families across 6 top results

How to remedy it

Group color/size/variant duplicates under a product-family cap for broad queries. Keep exact product searches variant-rich, but diversify broad discovery queries across families, brands, styles, and use cases.

Recommendations

Prioritized Roadmap

1

Implement brand priority boost for brand-specific queries

Fixes Brand & Model Search failures where customers searching "makita" or "flymo lawnmowers" see competitor brands instead of the requested brand.

Critical Quick Win
2

Add price constraint parsing for budget and price-anchored queries

Fixes Filters & Constraints failures where "under 300" and "cheap" queries ignore price limits entirely.

Critical Medium Effort
3

Configure semantic intent recognition for natural language and use case queries

Fixes Language Understanding failures where "how to clean my patio quickly" returns soup mugs instead of pressure washers.

Critical Major Project
4

Implement category mapping for broad terms like "tools" and "home"

Fixes Product Discovery failures where "tools" returns hot tubs and "home" returns only garden hoses.

Critical Medium Effort
5

Add seasonal and occasion-based product tagging system

Fixes Shopping Context failures where "father's day gifts" and "summer garden care" return irrelevant products.

Critical Major Project
6

Add an intent & boost layer before keyword ranking (brand, product-type, price, and bestseller signals)

Addresses the systematic ranking inversions across all capabilities where highest-scoring products appear at positions #9-#15.

Critical Medium Effort
7

Implement compound word detection for merged/split word queries

Fixes Typo Tolerance failures where "hedgetrimmers", "paintoffers", and "lawn mowers" are poorly ranked despite correct term recognition.

Moderate Quick Win
8

Add product type hierarchy for multi-attribute queries

Fixes cases where customers searching "dulux white paint" see 30ml testers before full-size paint containers.

Moderate Medium Effort
Industry Context

How You Compare

Metric Your Score Industry Target Source
Relevant result in top 3 53% 80%+ Baymard Institute
Average best result position #5 #1–2 Industry best practice
Irrelevant #1 result 15% <10% Industry best practice

70% of ecommerce sites cannot handle typos in their site search (Baymard Institute). Sites that do handle them gain a significant edge.

~30% of ecommerce visitors use site search, but they generate a disproportionate share of revenue — search users convert at 1.8–3x the rate of browse-only visitors (Econsultancy, Algolia).

31% of site searches end at a dead end — irrelevant results or zero results — across the average ecommerce site (Baymard Institute).

The top 3 results capture ~55–60% of all clicks. Results below position 3 are seen by a rapidly shrinking share of customers.

Revenue-Risk Failures

The queries most likely to cost sales

These examples show where the customer either sees no useful result, sees the wrong intent first, or has to dig past weaker results to find the right product.

#1 wood stain Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #15, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance product 'Ronseal 2.5 Litre Quick Drying Woodstain Satin Dark Oak' (0.844 relevance) was ranked #15 instead of #1. Meanwhile, lower relevance products like 'Ronseal 750ml Quick Drying Woodstain Ebony' (0.820 relevance) appeared at #1. The best result was displaced by 14 positions, and multiple products with 0.828+ relevance scores were ranked below products with 0.820 scores.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Customer saw

#1 0.82

Ronseal 750ml Quick Drying Woodstain Ebony €27.99

#2 0.83

Ronseal 750ml Quick Drying Woodstain Satin Mahogany €27.99

#3 0.82

Ronseal 10 Year Woodstain Antique Pine 2.5L €59.99

#4 0.84

Ronseal 10 Year Woodstain Dark Oak 2.5L €59.99

#5 0.83

Ronseal 2.5 Litre Quick Drying Woodstain Satin Mahogany €59.99

Should have seen

#15 0.84

Ronseal 2.5 Litre Quick Drying Woodstain Satin Dark Oak €59.99

#4 0.84

Ronseal 10 Year Woodstain Dark Oak 2.5L €59.99

#2 0.83

Ronseal 750ml Quick Drying Woodstain Satin Mahogany €27.99

#5 0.83

Ronseal 2.5 Litre Quick Drying Woodstain Satin Mahogany €59.99

#11 0.83

Ronseal 10 Year Woodstain Deep Mahogany 2.5L €59.99

#2 budget paint brushes Constraint dropped

The best matching result appeared at position #15, after weaker results.

Evidence

The query 'budget paint brushes' included a price constraint ('budget') but the search engine completely ignored it. The top 3 results are all expensive Rust-Oleum paint products at €33.99 and €18.49, while the actual budget paint brushes like 'Ultimate 2in Wall & Ceiling Blade Paint Brush' (€10.99) and 'Fleetwood Expert Paint Brush 3"' (€11.99) were buried at positions #13 and #11. The best matching result 'Fleetwood 3" Pro D Paint Brush' at €13.49 was pushed to position #15.

Fix

Extract the constraint in the query and enforce it in ranking so brand, attribute, price, or use-case requirements are not ignored.

Customer saw

#1 0.48

Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Cobalt Blue €33.99

#2 0.48

Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Cobalt Blue €18.49

#3 0.48

Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Racing Green €18.49

#4 0.49

Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Espresso Brown €33.99

#5 0.49

Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Slate Grey €33.99

Should have seen

#15 0.61

Fleetwood 3" Pro D Paint Brush €13.49

#13 0.60

Ultimate 2in Wall & Ceiling Blade Paint Brush €10.99

#10 0.59

MX 75mm Professional Paint Brush €14.99

#11 0.58

Fleetwood Expert Paint Brush 3" €11.99

#14 0.57

MX 100mm Professional Paint Brush €17.99

#3 garden Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #15, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance result '750Ml Garden And Wood Paint Juniper Burst' (0.590 relevance) was buried at position #15, while lower relevance items like 'Ronseal 750ml Ultimate Hardwood Garden Furniture Stain Natural Cedar' (0.527 relevance) and 'Ronseal 750ml Ultimate Hardwood Garden Furniture Stain English Oak' (0.523 relevance) occupied the top positions. The search engine retrieved relevant garden products but completely inverted the ranking order.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Customer saw

#1 0.53

Ronseal 750ml Ultimate Hardwood Garden Furniture Stain Natural Cedar €28.99

#4 hedgetrimmers Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search engine correctly identified hedge trimmers but ranked them poorly. The best result '420W Electric Hedge Trimmer, 45cm Cutting Length' (relevance 0.828) was buried at position #14, while two brushcutters with much lower relevance scores (0.420 and 0.443) occupied positions #1 and #3. Multiple hedge trimmers with high relevance scores (0.816) were scattered throughout positions #4-#15 instead of being grouped at the top.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Customer saw

PXC18V Crdls Brshlss Brushcutter 25.5-30cm Cut Wdt €274.95

#5 0.82

#5 black+decker Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'Black+Decker 300W Oscillating Multi Tool & Accessories' (0.816 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower relevance results like 'Black & Decker 2.2KW Smart Convection Turbo Heater' (0.699 relevance) appeared at position #3. Multiple high-scoring Black+Decker products with 0.785+ relevance scores were pushed down to positions #11-14, while products with 0.688-0.699 relevance occupied the top 5 positions.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#6 electric hedge trimmer lightweight Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'Einhell GC-CH 18/40 Li Solo Cordless Hedge Trimmer' (0.688 relevance) was buried at position #14, while much lower relevance results like '420W Electric Hedge Trimmer, 45cm Cutting Length' (0.641 relevance) and '900W Electric HighReach Hedge Trimmer,42cm Cut Lnt' (0.637 relevance) occupied the top positions. The top 3 positions had an average relevance of only 0.642, significantly below the best available result.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Detailed Analysis

Product Discovery

5 examples
"garden"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #15

The highest relevance result '750Ml Garden And Wood Paint Juniper Burst' (0.590 relevance) was buried at position #15, while lower relevance items like 'Ronseal 750ml Ultimate Hardwood Garden Furniture Stain Natural Cedar' (0.527 relevance) and 'Ronseal 750ml Ultimate Hardwood Garden Furniture Stain English Oak' (0.523 relevance) occupied the top positions. The search engine retrieved relevant garden products but completely inverted the ranking order.

Customer Saw

  1. Ronseal 750ml Ultimate Hardwood Garden Furniture Stain Natural Cedar (€28.99)
  2. Ronseal 750ml Ultimate Hardwood Garden Furniture Stain English Oak (€28.99)
  3. Johnstone's Garden Colours Vintage Rose 2.5L (€23.19)
  4. Peckish Secret Garden Nyjer Feeder (€16.99)
  5. Garden Taps - Cam Lock Tap Adaptor (€12.69)

Should Have Seen

  1. 750Ml Garden And Wood Paint Juniper Burst (€16.99)
  2. Stone Jane Statue Garden Ornament 60cm (€49.99)
  3. Garden Taps - Cam Lock Tap Adaptor (€12.69)

Best match was buried at position #15.

"bosch garden powertools"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #9

The highest relevance result 'Bosch Isio Shape & Edge 3.6V Cordless Grass and Shrub Shear' (0.676 relevance) was buried at position #9, while lower-relevance items like 'Bosch Cordless Chainsaw 18v' (0.609) and 'Bosch UniversalChain 35cm 1800W Electric Chainsaw' (0.598) occupied the top positions. Multiple high-relevance cordless garden tools were pushed down: 'Bosch Rotak 18V-32 Cordless Lawnmower' (0.605) at #12 and 'Bosch EasyGrassCut 18-230 2Ah Cordless GrassTrimmer' (0.605) at #13.

Customer Saw

  1. Bosch Cordless Chainsaw 18v (€259.99)
  2. Bosch UniversalChain 35cm 1800W Electric Chainsaw (€159.99)
  3. Bosch AXT 2200 Rapid Shredder (€329.99)
  4. Bosch ART 30 Grass Trimmer (€109.99)
  5. Bosch VisiMow18V-100 Cordless Lawnmower (€499.99)

Should Have Seen

  1. Bosch Isio Shape & Edge 3.6V Cordless Grass and Shrub Shear (€84.99)
  2. Bosch Cordless Chainsaw 18v (€259.99)
  3. Bosch Rotak 18V-32 Cordless Lawnmower (€259.99)

Best match was buried at position #9.

"flymo lawnmowers"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #9

Customer searched for 'flymo lawnmowers' but the only Flymo product (Flymo EasiLife Go 400 Robotic Lawnmower with relevance 0.852) was buried at position #9. The top 8 positions were dominated by other brands: Hyundai (positions #1, #3), Honda (#2), Bosch (#4, #5, #8), and Pro Lawn (#6, #7). These non-Flymo results had much lower relevance scores (0.457-0.494) than the actual Flymo product.

The engine treated 'home' as a keyword match in product titles rather than mapping it to the home product category.

Typo Tolerance

Merged words · Critical · Best result at position #14

The best result '420W Electric Hedge Trimmer, 45cm Cutting Length' (relevance 0.828) was buried at position #14, while two brushcutters with much lower relevance scores (0.420 and 0.443) occupied positions #1 and #3. Filters & Constraints

5 examples
"budget paint brushes"
Price anchored · Critical · Best result at position #15

The query 'budget paint brushes' included a price constraint ('budget') but the search engine completely ignored it. The top 3 results are all expensive Rust-Oleum paint products at €33.99 and €18.49, while the actual budget paint brushes like 'Ultimate 2in Wall & Ceiling Blade Paint Brush' (€10.99) and 'Fleetwood Expert Paint Brush 3"' (€11.99) were buried at positions #13 and #11. The best matching result 'Fleetwood 3" Pro D Paint Brush' at €13.49 was pushed to position #15.

  1. Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Cobalt Blue (€33.99)
  2. Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Cobalt Blue (€18.49)
  3. Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Racing Green (€18.49)
  4. Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Espresso Brown (€33.99)
  5. Rust-Oleum Universal All-Surface Paint (Brush) Slate Grey (€33.99)

Should Have Seen

  1. Fleetwood 3" Pro D Paint Brush (€13.49)
  2. Ultimate 2in Wall & Ceiling Blade Paint Brush (€10.99)
  3. MX 75mm Professional Paint Brush (€14.99)

Best match was buried at position #15.

"electric hedge trimmer lightweight"
Multi-attribute · Critical · Best result at position #14

The highest relevance result 'Einhell GC-CH 18/40 Li Solo Cordless Hedge Trimmer' (0.688 relevance) was buried at position #14, while much lower relevance results like '420W Electric Hedge Trimmer, 45cm Cutting Length' (0.641 relevance) and '900W Electric HighReach Hedge Trimmer,42cm Cut Lnt' (0.637 relevance) occupied the top positions. The top 3 positions had an average relevance of only 0.642, significantly below the best available result.

Customer Saw

  1. 420W Electric Hedge Trimmer, 45cm Cutting Length (€99.95)
  2. 900W Electric HighReach Hedge Trimmer,42cm Cut Lnt (€184.95)
  3. PXC 18V Crdless Hedge Trimmer 40cm Cut Lng 1x2.0Ah (€149.95)
  4. PXC 36V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,61cm Cutting Length (€209.95)
  5. PXC 36V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,65cm Cutting Length (€224.95)

Should Have Seen

  1. Einhell GC-CH 18/40 Li Solo Cordless Hedge Trimmer (€49.99)
  2. PXC 18V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,50cm Cutting Length (€124.95)
  3. PXC 18V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,46cm Cutting Length (€104.95)

Best match was buried at position #14.

"lawnmower under 300"
Price anchored · Critical · Best result at position #7

The top 2 results violate the price constraint: #1 'Pro Lawn Self Propelled Electric Start Hyundai Lawnmower 53cm' at €449.99 and #2 'GCV170 Honda Petrol Lawnmower 51 CM 166CC' at €499.99 are both well above the €300 limit. Additionally, results #10, #12, #14, and #15 are priced at €469.99, €409.99, €369.99, and €599.99 respectively, all exceeding the constraint. Meanwhile, 8 products under €300 with high relevance scores (0.750-0.852) are ranked lower than these overpriced items.

Customer Saw

  1. Pro Lawn Self Propelled Electric Start Hyundai Lawnmower 53cm (€449.99)
  2. GCV170 Honda Petrol Lawnmower 51 CM 166CC (€499.99)
  3. Pro-Lawn Hyundai Powered Petrol Lawnmower 42cm 139cc (€279.99)
  4. Bosch Rotak 18V-32 Cordless Lawnmower (€259.99)
  5. Bosch UniversalRotak 37-565 Lawnmower (€199.99)

Should Have Seen

  1. Pro Lawn 46CM Self Propelled Petrol Lawn Mower 146CC (€299.99)
  2. Bosch UniversalRotak 37-565 Lawnmower (€199.99)
  3. Pro-Lawn Hyundai Powered Petrol Lawnmower 42cm 139cc (€279.99)

Best match was buried at position #7.

"cheap hedge trimmer"
Price anchored · Critical · Best result at position #5

The query 'cheap hedge trimmer' includes a clear price constraint, but the engine completely ignored it. The most expensive item (Stradbally Ladder - 8 Step Tripod Hedge Trimmer at €416.99) was ranked #1, while the cheapest and most relevant items were buried: the €99.95 Electric Hedge Trimmer was ranked #13, and the €104.95 PXC 18V Cordless model was ranked #9. The engine appears to be ranking purely on product title relevance without any consideration of the 'cheap' price constraint.

Customer Saw

  1. Stradbally Ladder - 8 Step Tripod Hedge Trimmer (€416.99)
  2. PXC 18V Crdless Hedge Trimmer 40cm Cut Lng 1x2.0Ah (€149.95)
  3. PXC 36V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,61cm Cutting Length (€209.95)
  4. PXC 36V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,65cm Cutting Length (€224.95)
  5. PXC 18V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,50cm Cutting Length (€124.95)

Should Have Seen

  1. PXC 18V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,50cm Cutting Length (€124.95)
  2. PXC 18V Cordless Hedge Trimmer,46cm Cutting Length (€104.95)
  3. 900W Electric HighReach Hedge Trimmer,42cm Cut Lnt (€184.95)

Best match was buried at position #5.

"bosch cordless garden tools"
Multi-attribute · Moderate · Best result at position #6

The search engine retrieved all relevant Bosch cordless garden tools but ranked them incorrectly. The highest relevance product 'Bosch Isio Shape & Edge 3.6V Cordless Grass and Shrub Shear' (0.824) was buried at position #6, while the lowest relevance product 'Bosch Cordless Chainsaw 18v' (0.766) was ranked #1. The top-performing 'Bosch EasyGrassCut 18-230 2Ah Cordless GrassTrimmer' (0.809) was only at position #4.

Customer Saw

  1. Bosch Cordless Chainsaw 18v (€259.99)
  2. Bosch VisiMow18V-100 Cordless Lawnmower (€499.99)
  3. Bosch Rotak 18V-32 Cordless Lawnmower (€259.99)
  4. Bosch EasyGrassCut 18-230 2Ah Cordless GrassTrimmer (€149.99)
  5. Bosch Universal Hedge Cut 18-55 Cordless Hedgecutter (€204.99)

Should Have Seen

  1. Bosch Isio Shape & Edge 3.6V Cordless Grass and Shrub Shear (€84.99)
  2. Bosch EasyGrassCut 18-230 2Ah Cordless GrassTrimmer (€149.99)
  3. Bosch Rotak 18V-32 Cordless Lawnmower (€259.99)

Best match was buried at position #6.

Positive Findings

What's Working

  • Retrieval is working — relevant results were found in 35 of 60 queries. The primary issue is ranking, not finding: the right results exist in the result set but aren't being surfaced at the top.
  • Close to correct on 28 queries ("dulux paint", "colourtrend", "dulx paint"): the best result was within the top 3, just not at #1.
  • Your search correctly interprets typos and spelling variations — when we searched with misspellings, it recognized what customers meant. The issue is ranking the corrected results, not recognising the typo.
Full Data

Scope and Boundaries

What this audit covers

Included

Search relevance, ranking quality, retrieval gaps, query interpretation failures, visible result ordering, and prioritized fixes based on the tested query set.

Not included

Implementation work, platform configuration changes, merchandising strategy, analytics instrumentation, or a full conversion-rate analysis beyond the search result experience.

Next Steps

We'd welcome the opportunity to walk you through these findings in detail and discuss a remediation plan tailored to your team's priorities and timeline.

The issues identified in this audit are fixable — most ecommerce search platforms support the capabilities tested here, and the roadmap above is ordered by impact to help you prioritize.

Schedule a 30-Minute Walkthrough
